text.title: Next.js App Router Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying nextjs-app-router. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Page declares 'use client' at the top; all children rendered client-side; SEO + paint metrics regress.
antipattern.root-cause: Author added 'use client' to use one hook; cascading effect not noticed.
antipattern.fix: Move 'use client' to the smallest leaf component that needs it. Verify SEO bots see server-rendered HTML.

antipattern.symptom: Client component fetches data on mount; user sees spinner; LCP regresses.
antipattern.root-cause: Pages Router habit; server-component data passing not adopted.
antipattern.fix: Refactor: parent server component fetches; passes data as prop. Client component renders. SWR allowed only for live/interactive cases.

antipattern.symptom: Mutation flows via /api/orders POST + fetch; CSRF + revalidation hand-rolled and inconsistent.
antipattern.fix: Migrate to server actions. Form posts directly to the action; revalidatePath/Tag declared inline.

antipattern.symptom: Customer-facing prices stale because default fetch cache cached the response indefinitely.
antipattern.root-cause: No revalidate declared; default behaviour kicked in.
antipattern.fix: Add explicit `next: { revalidate: 60, tags: ['prices'] }` to fetch. Use revalidateTag from server action when prices change.
